safe_claim: Candidate Linux source surfaces are mapped for future opaque monitor receipt consumption or explicit exclusion, without adding Linux code, ABI, tracepoints, runtime observation, monitor verification, authority, or protection claims.
forbidden_claim: Any mapped Linux source surface currently consumes or mints monitor receipts, approves direct-call ABI, changes scheduler behavior, verifies monitor behavior, or provides production protection.

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Opaque authority names are visible to Linux builds, but no receipt layout or consumer exists.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Forward declarations are not monitor receipts.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: opaque type names treated as monitor-minted receipt state
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep type-only until a separate patch gate approves concrete receipt carriers

receipt_consumer_rows.symbol_or_pattern: This translation unit is intentionally inert
receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Potential future internal home for receipt-shadow helpers, currently inert.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: A helper file is not receipt authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: inert file treated as direct-call implementation evidence
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: do not add helpers until receipt lifecycle and failure semantics are gated

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Build visibility for current inert CapSched translation unit.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Kbuild inclusion is not receipt consumption or behavior approval.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: CONFIG_CAPSCHED build visibility treated as direct-call carrier approval
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep build gate separate from behavior-changing patch gates

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Potential future frozen-shadow check before queued runnable state, not a monitor call or receipt mint.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Runqueue insertion cannot create or refresh monitor authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: enqueue success treated as EntryResultReceipt or response authority
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: if considered later, model hot-path check as generation comparison only

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Wakeup admission edge where stale receipt shadows would be dangerous if not frozen before runnable transition.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Wakeup state changes do not prove monitor approval.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: successful wakeup treated as renewed response or revoke completion
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep as TOCTOU pressure point for future model before patching

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Potential future fast recheck that a pre-frozen runnable use has not gone stale.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Pick-next must not perform heavyweight receipt lookup or mint a response.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: pick result treated as monitor receipt or schema acceptance
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep any future check bounded to live generation/epoch comparison

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Potential future Domain/MemoryView activation adjacency; currently only a Linux scheduler switch path.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Context switch does not make wrapper return or transport completion monitor approval.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: context switch completion treated as monitor activation receipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: model monitor activation receipt separately before any hook placement

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Main scheduling state machine where future direct-call effects would be highly visible and high risk.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: __schedule state transitions are not monitor receipt creation.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: schedule path return or trace event treated as runtime coverage or monitor approval
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: prefer no direct-call work here unless a model proves bounded non-authoritative checks

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Existing user sched_attr copy/compatibility shape is a policy input analog, not a canonical monitor request image.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: copy_struct_from_user result cannot become RequestImageReceipt.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: Linux-copied sched_attr treated as monitor canonical request
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: map future CapSched request copy separately from existing sched_attr ABI

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Existing scheduler policy mutation path and privilege checks are future policy-front-end context.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: CAP_SYS_NICE, LSM, and policy success are not monitor schema acceptance.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: Linux scheduler policy success treated as SchemaReceipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep Linux policy and monitor schema acceptance as separate authorities

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Existing affinity request path is a future request-shaping analog for CPU-placement constraints.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: User cpumask and permission success cannot mint monitor CPU or Domain receipts.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: sched_setaffinity success treated as monitor CPU-affinity authority
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: map future allowed-cpu receipt fields separately from user cpumask ABI

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Existing cpuset intersection and affinity application path shows policy narrowing pressure.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: cpuset intersection is not monitor MemoryView, Domain, or CPU budget authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: Linux cpuset result treated as monitor-owned allowed CPU receipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: preserve cpuset as policy input before monitor-owned budget/affinity receipt

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Task identity creation and generation propagation boundary for future SpawnCap/receipt freshness.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: fork/clone inheritance cannot amplify direct-call or spawn authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: duplicated task_struct state treated as inherited monitor receipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: model receipt and generation inheritance before task layout changes

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: io_uring worker creation is a provenance-sensitive lifecycle edge, not a generic receipt inheritance path.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: PF_IO_WORKER/PF_USER_WORKER identity is not caller Domain authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: io_uring worker creation treated as caller receipt propagation
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: require typed io_uring carrier model before any direct-call integration

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Exec is a generation/freshness boundary where Domain identity must not be silently changed.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: exec success or credential transition is not Domain receipt renewal.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: exec treated as Domain switch or receipt refresh
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep exec Domain invariance and stale-shadow invalidation explicit in later model

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Task teardown and cleanup boundary where future shadows must be invalidated or released.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Linux task exit cleanup is not monitor revoke completion.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: do_exit path treated as RevokeReceipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: separate self-exit cleanup from external ThreadControlCap/revoke receipt semantics

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Generic queueing is an exclusion anchor: repeated submissions may coalesce while pending.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: work_struct pending state cannot store caller-specific receipt authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: caller receipt overwritten in reusable work_struct after queue_work coalescing
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: Domain-originated async work needs a typed carrier instead of generic workqueue mutation

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Generic worker execution is an exclusion anchor: authority naturally runs in worker/kthread context.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: worker task identity is not original caller authority.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: worker->current_func(work) treated as caller-authorized direct-call response consumer
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: require capsched_work carrier and service/caller intersection for Domain-originated work

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Direct evidence that work executes through the worker's current function pointer, not original caller context.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Callback invocation does not carry caller Domain receipt by default.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: generic work callback treated as preserving caller receipt provenance
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep as negative source anchor for async provenance modeling

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: io_uring worker loop is an exclusion anchor until per-request provenance is modeled.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: io-wq worker execution cannot be ambient authority for the submitting Domain.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: io_wq worker loop treated as preserving direct-call receipt authority
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: model io_uring registered resources and worker carriers before patching

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Still-absent internal request-envelope builder candidate.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Monitor must own canonical request image, bounded copy, digest, replay key, and caller epoch.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: Linux-built envelope treated as canonical monitor request
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: define monitor request-copy semantics before any helper stub

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Still-absent direct-call wrapper and arch backend candidate.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Monitor entry result must be monitor-minted and cannot be inferred from wrapper return.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: transport completion or wrapper success treated as EntryResultReceipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: define arch-independent and arch-specific clobber/failure contract before stubs

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Still-absent internal schema query/cache candidate.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Only the monitor accepts schema versions and critical-field policy.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: Linux-visible schema cache treated as SchemaReceipt
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: define schema cache as non-authoritative derived shadow only

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Still-absent response-handle shadow/cache candidate.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Response handles and shadow generations must be monitor-minted.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: timeout, retry, or cached shadow refresh treated as renewed authority
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: model shadow lifetime and invalidate-before-reuse ordering before storage design

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Still-absent internal control/revoke lane candidate.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Monitor owns revoke acceptance, response embargo, and completion receipt.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: Linux control priority treated as bypassing replay, budget, or epoch
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: define revoke lane ordering and in-flight response rule before stubs

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Still-absent test-only failure injection surface.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Failure injection cannot affect production monitor or Linux decisions.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: test hook treated as live control path or receipt source
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: keep failure injection behind future KUnit/test-only gate

receipt_consumer_rows.candidate_role: Trace-only observation remains a plan until a separate privileged runbook executes.
receipt_consumer_rows.authority_boundary: Trace observation cannot be receipt authority or runtime coverage until separately executed and still remains non-authoritative.
receipt_consumer_rows.forbidden_upgrade: trace plan treated as runtime coverage, public tracepoint ABI, or monitor verification
receipt_consumer_rows.next_step: only build tracefs runbook if later validation needs runtime observation

excluded_surface_rules: Generic workqueue carrier state must not be retrofitted as caller receipt authority.
io_uring worker identity must not be treated as caller Domain authority.
scheduler hot paths may at most consume pre-frozen generation or epoch shadows until a stronger model approves more.
policy syscalls remain policy inputs and compatibility surfaces, not monitor receipt roots.
acceptance_checks: every current candidate row has a source_path and symbol_or_pattern
future gaps remain explicit gap rows
generic async rows are excluded or dangerous, not approved receipt consumers
no row claims authority, monitor verification, behavior change, ABI, runtime coverage, or protection
